Treatment of Trees LV 535
Being sown thereafter in a soil soaked with hail-water, they will
bear fruit in a day.
[Bhatfotpala says that the seeds smeared with buffalo-dung
should be kept inside a heap of dry buffalo-dung in a pot until the
contents are drenched by hail-water. He construes as
here too. The author enumerates the asterisms suitable
for planting trees,.
The asterisms prescriSpd by the Seers of transcendental wisdom
(such as Garga) for the planting of trees are the three Uttaras,
Rohini, Anuradha, Citra, Mfga^irsa, RevatX, Mula, Vi^akha, Pusya,
Sravana, AivinX and Hasta.

Utpala.]

Chapter LVI.—Description of Temples.
sr^rcf HftrtWRmrq W^r i
^crnTcT^ n?n
One should build a temple in order to enhance one's fame
and religious merit, after securing an abundant supply of water
through tanks and lakes, laying out fine gardens.
